questions.question: You're a team leader at a marketing firm, and one of your team members, John, has been consistently late to team meetings and project deadlines. This has started to impact the team's overall performance and morale. The client is also beginning to notice the delays and is expressing concern about meeting the project timeline. You've tried talking to John about it informally, but the issue persists. What would you do?
options: Assign some of John's tasks to other team members to ensure the project stays on track.
Document John's performance issues and consider disciplinary action.
Have a formal meeting with John to discuss the impact of his tardiness and set clear expectations.
Ignore the issue and hope it resolves itself.
questions.explanation: Most Likely Response should be option C: Have a formal meeting with John to discuss the impact of his tardiness and set clear expectations. A formal meeting allows for a structured discussion about the issue, setting clear expectations for improvement, and understanding any underlying reasons for John's tardiness. Ignoring the issue could lead to further delays and negatively impact the team's morale and performance.

questions.question: In a certain code language ‘TRUMBLE’ is coded as ‘XTUMHNC’, ‘STUPIDS’ is coded as ‘XVTPVFJ’. In the same language how ‘MUSCLES’ be coded.

questions.explanation: Study of animal = Zoology Study of fossil = Palaeontology > जानवरों का अध्ययन = प्राणि विज्ञान जीवाश्म का अध्ययन = जीवाश्म विज्ञान

questions.question: Given below is a statement followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Read the statement carefully and decide which of the conclusions are being followed by the given statement: Statement: A community organization launches a program to provide free and healthy meals to homeless individuals. However, the program does not address underlying housing issues. Conclusions: I. Homelessness rates will decrease. II. Nutrition and health outcomes will improve. III. Community engagement and social cohesion will increase.
options: Only conclusions I and II follow
Only conclusions I and III follow
Only conclusions II and III follow
questions.explanation: Providing healthy meals can improve nutrition and health. Community engagement and social cohesion may increase. Hence, conclusions II and III follow.

questions.question: A is the husband of B, who is the mother of C. Q is the husband of T, who is the sister of R. C is the mother of R. How is ‘C’ related to ‘Q’? > A, B का पति है, जो C की माँ है। Q, T का पति है, जो R की बहन है। C, R की माँ है। ‘C’ का ‘Q’ से क्या संबंध है?
questions.explanation: [C] Mother – in – law > [C] सास

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that, when placed from left to right in the following blanks, will complete the letter-series S __ U __ L S __ U __P L __ S__U __ __ S S W __ P __ S ? > उस विकल्प का चयन करें जो उन अक्षरों को दर्शाता है, जो निम्नलिखित रिक्त स्थानों में बाएं से दाएं रखने पर अक्षर-श्रृंखला को पूरा करेंगे S __ U __ L S __ U __P L __ S__U __ __ S S W __ P __ S ?
questions.explanation: TPSUSVPLUL > TPSUSVPLUL

questions.question: The cricket stadium you're managing is hosting a high-profile match, and a sudden rainstorm is forecasted to hit just before the start of play. The grounds staff are working hard to dry the pitch, but it's clear that the outfield will be wet and potentially hazardous for fielding. The match referee is considering whether to delay the start or call off the match altogether. Which of the following factors should the referee consider when making this decision? (I) The safety of the players and spectators. (II) The potential impact on the tournament schedule and broadcasting commitments. (III) The condition of the pitch and the outfield.
questions.explanation: All of the above. Reasoning: The referee needs to balance player safety, the integrity of the match, and logistical considerations. Delaying or calling off the match might be necessary to ensure safety and fair play.

What was the day of the week on 12 November, 1996?
questions.explanation: 12 November, 1996 Sum of date + last two digit of the year = 108 Quotient when last two digit of the year is divided by 4 = 96/4 = 24 Year code = 0 and month code = 3 Now, = 2 remainder = 2 is code for tuesday.

questions.question: Three statements are given, followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follows from the statements? Statements: All Balloons are Green. Some Greens are White. No Whites are Black. Conclusion: I. All Green can be Black. II. All Balloons are White.
questions.explanation: I. False – As same part of green that is white can never be black II. False – Not given in the statement.

questions.explanation: Required common area = 11 > आवश्यक सामान्य क्षेत्र = 11

How many meaningful English words can be formed with the second, fourth, sixth and seventh letters of the word TROUBLEMAKER [when counted from left to right] using each letter only once in each word?
questions.explanation: second, fourth, sixth and seventh letters of the word TROUBLEMAKER = R, U, L and E Using these letters, we can make two words = RULE and LURE

questions.question: In which city of Madhya Pradesh did the global investors summit 2025 take place?
questions.explanation: The Global Investors Summit 2025 was held in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, on February 24-25, 2025. The summit aimed to attract domestic and international investments in sectors like manufacturing, IT, tourism, and renewable energy. It featured participation from global business leaders, policymakers, and industry experts to boost economic growth in the state.

questions.question: Consider the following statements and select the correct answer from the codes given below: 1) Subhash Chandra Bose formed the Forward Block. 2) Bhagat Singh was one of the founders of Hindustan Republican Association. Code:
questions.explanation: Subhash Chandra Bose resigned from the Presidentship of Congress and founded “Forward Block’ on 3rd May 1939. Hindustan Republican Association (HRA) was originally founded in 1924 by revolutionaries like Ram Prasad Bismil, Sachindra Nath Sanyal, and Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee, not by Chandra Shekhar Azad or Bhagat Singh.

questions.question: Which of the following Ashokan rock edicts declares prohibition of animal sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Major Rock Edict 1 of Ashoka focuses on prohibiting animal sacrifice and reducing the slaughter of animals for food. It highlights Ashoka's commitment to Dhamma [moral law] and promotes compassion towards all living beings. The edict mentions that the king has stopped royal hunting and the killing of specific animals in his household. It reflects Ashoka's efforts to spread non-violence [Ahimsa] and ethical governance.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles was amended in the Constitution [Fifth Amendment Act], 1955?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Amendment Act of 1955 amended Article 3 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the formation of new states and changes to existing state boundaries. This amendment introduced a provision requiring the President to seek the opinion of the concerned state legislature before introducing any bill in Parliament related to altering state boundaries. However, the opinion of the state legislature was not binding on Parliament. This change ensured that states had a consultative role in territorial changes while maintaining Parliament’s authority over reorganization matters.

questions.question: Examine the following statements and select the correct answer by using codes given below: 1. Devprayag is located at the confluence of the Bhagirathi and Alaknanda rivers. 2. Rudraprayag is located at the confluence of the Mandakini and Alaknanda rivers. 3. Alaknanda flows through the town of Badrinath. 4. Kedarnath is considered as the biggest seat established by Adi Shankaracharya.
questions.explanation: Badrinath was established as a major pilgrimage site by Adi Shankaracharya, not Kedarnath. It is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva in India. Kedarnath is known as Samadhi Sthal of Adi Sankaracharya. Hence, statement (4) is wrong.

questions.question: Which non-essential amino acid is synthesised by the hydroxylation of phenylalanine in a reaction catalysed by phenylalanine hydroxylase?
questions.explanation: Tyrosine is a non-essential amino acid synthesized from phenylalanine through a hydroxylation reaction. This process is catalyzed by the enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase, which requires tetrahydrobiopterin [BH₄] as a cofactor.

questions.question: 'The forty minutes raga’ is an album composed by?
questions.explanation: The album "The 40-Minute Raga" was composed by Ali Akbar Khan. Ali Akbar Khan was a legendary sarod maestro and one of the greatest Indian classical musicians. He was known for his long, elaborate renditions of ragas, and "The 40-Minute Raga" is one of his most famous recordings. This album showcases his mastery of the sarod and his deep understanding of Indian classical music, particularly in the Maihar Gharana tradition.

questions.question: Who among the following wrote the book ‘Chanakya’s Chant’?
questions.explanation: The book "Chanakya’s Chant" was written by Ashwin Sanghi. Published in 2010, Chanakya’s Chant is a historical fiction novel that intertwines two parallel stories—one set in ancient India during the time of Chanakya, and the other in modern India, following a shrewd political strategist. The book highlights political manoeuvring, strategy, and power dynamics, drawing parallels between the two timelines. Ashwin Sanghi is known for blending history with contemporary fiction, making complex subjects engaging for readers.

questions.question: Identify the items included in the Directive Principles of State Policy. 1. Prohibition of Consumption of Liquor. 2. Prohibition of employment of children in factories or mines. 3. Prohibition of beggar or forced labour. 4. Prohibition of untouchability. Select the correct answer with the help of the code given below:
questions.explanation: Article 47 of the Directive Principles of State Policy underscores that it is the duty of the State to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living and to improve public health. The State shall endeavour to bring about prohibition on the consumption except for medicinal purposes of intoxicating drinks and of drugs which are injurious to health, whereas prohibition of employment of children in factories or mines, prohibition of beggar or forced labour, and prohibition of untouchability are mentioned under Article 24, 23 and 17 respectively of Fundamental rights.

questions.question: Recently, which state government has announced to provide additional five kilograms of rice under Anna Bhagya Scheme?
questions.explanation: The Karnataka government has announced that, starting February 2025, it will provide an additional 5 kilograms of rice per person under the Anna Bhagya scheme, replacing the previous cash transfer of ₹170 per beneficiary. This decision follows the Union government's commitment to supply the required rice quantities to the state. Initially, due to procurement challenges, the state offered cash in lieu of rice to Below Poverty Line [BPL] families.

questions.question: Proposed by Lord Lytton, then viceroy of India [governed 1876-80], the act was intended to prevent press from expressing criticism of British policies-notably, the opposition that had grown with the _____ [1878-80]?
questions.explanation: The Second Anglo-Afghan War [1878–1880] was fought between British India and Afghanistan, as Britain aimed to counter Russian influence in the region. During this period, Indian newspapers became highly critical of British actions, prompting Lord Lytton to introduce the Vernacular Press Act of 1878 to curb dissent. This act allowed the government to censor vernacular newspapers that criticized British rule, significantly restricting freedom of the press in India.

questions.question: Consider the following statements? Sodium silicate and sodium sulphate are mixed as detergents in washing powders so that: 1. To maintain the dryness of the powder. 2. To maintain the basicity of powder. Which of the following statement/s is/are correct?
questions.explanation: By adding sodium sulphate to the washing powder, it increases the basicity of the powder. The addition of sodium silicate into the washing powder turns the powder crystalline, so that the powder stays in dry form.

questions.question: Which region in India receives less rainfall in summer, but heavy rainfall in winter season due to the retreating north easterly winds?
questions.explanation: The Eastern Coastal Region of India receives less rainfall in summer but heavy rainfall in winter due to the retreating northeasterly winds. During summer, the southwest monsoon brings most of the rainfall to India, but this region, especially Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and parts of Odisha, remains relatively dry. However, in winter [October to December], the northeast monsoon winds pick up moisture from the Bay of Bengal, causing heavy rainfall, particularly in Tamil Nadu, which receives over 50% of its annual rainfall during this season.

questions.question: Who among the following is associated with the age of consent act 1891 which raised the minimum age of consent for marriage to 12 years?
questions.explanation: The Age of Consent Act, 1891, was associated with B.M. Malabari, a social reformer and journalist. He played a key role in advocating against child marriage and for raising the minimum age of consent. His efforts influenced the British government to pass this law, which made it illegal for a girl under 12 years to be married or have marital relations.

questions.question: How many types of makeup [vesham] are used for the performers of Kathakali dance?
questions.explanation: The Kathakali dance performance uses five main types of makeup, known as Pachcha, Kathi, Kari, Thaadi, and Minukku. Each makeup type in Kathakali represents different character traits: Pachcha (Green): Noble and heroic characters like gods and kings. Kathi (Knife): Villains and anti-heroes with arrogance and power. Kari (Black): Demonesses and evil female characters. Thaadi (Beard): Divided into three types—Red (aggressive), White (divine), and Black (forest dwellers/demons). Minukku (Radiant): Gentle, spiritual, or female characters.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one is labelled as Assersion (A) and the other as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The Mansabdari system existed during the Mughal period. Reason (R): Mansabdars were selected on the basis of qualification. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
options: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: The Mansabdari system was the administrative system of the Mughal Empire. It determined the rank of government officials. Mansabdars were appointed based on their qualifications, and the king himself made these appointments. Hence, both the Assertion (A) and the Reason (R) are correct, but Reason (R) does not explain Assertion (A). This is because the Mansabdari system was designed primarily for administrative efficiency and military organization, not solely as a method to recruit qualified individuals.

questions.question: North India's first 1-megawatt green hydrogen plant has started in which state?
questions.explanation: North India's first 1-megawatt green hydrogen plant has been initiated in Himachal Pradesh. On February 5, 2025, Chief Minister Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu laid the foundation stone for this pioneering facility at Dabhota in Nalagarh Tehsil, Solan district. The project, a collaboration between Himachal Pradesh Power Corporation Limited [HPPCL] and Oil India Limited, is set to be completed within a year at an estimated cost of ₹9.04 crore. This initiative aligns with the state's ambition to become India's first green energy state by March 2026.

questions.question: In the context of cell organelles, a centriole is:
options: a heterogeneous family of organelles found ubiquitously in plant cells
a membrane-bound cell organelle that contains digestive enzymes
a paired barrel-shaped organelle located in the cytoplasm of animal cells near the nuclear envelope
a stack of small flat sacs formed by membranes inside the cell's cytoplasm
questions.explanation: A centriole is a paired, barrel-shaped organelle located in the cytoplasm of animal cells near the nuclear envelope. Centrioles play a crucial role in cell division by organizing the mitotic spindle and are involved in the formation of cilia and flagella. They are composed of microtubules arranged in a 9+0 structure and are typically found in animal cells but not in most plant cells. During cell division, centrioles help in the proper segregation of chromosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following is an inosilicate amphibole mineral used in highway construction and as railroad ballast?
questions.explanation: Hornblende is an inosilicate mineral commonly found in igneous and metamorphic rocks. Due to its hardness and durability, it is used in highway construction and as railroad ballast to provide stability and support. Its resistance to weathering and heavy loads makes it suitable for infrastructure projects like roads and railways.

questions.question: According to the Census of India 2011, which state has the highest number of slum population?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra recorded around 1.18 crore people living in slums, the highest among all states. Major cities like Mumbai, Pune, and Nagpur have significant slum populations, with Dharavi in Mumbai being one of the largest slums in Asia. The high urban migration and lack of affordable housing contribute to the large slum population in the state.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about mosquitoes : 1. Only males suck blood 2. Only females suck blood 3. Males have bigger wings than females 4. Females have bigger wings than males Which of these statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Only female mosquitoes suck the blood of warm-blooded vertebrate birds and mammals and live on them as an ectoparasite. The male mosquitoes do not suck blood, they survive by feeding on flowers and fruit juices. Occasionally, females may also suck juices. Generally, female mosquitoes are larger than male mosquitoes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about planet Saturn is true?
options: Satellites like Enceladus and Titan of Saturn are home to internal oceans, could possibly support life
Saturn is the fourth planet from the Sun and the largest planet in our solar system.
Saturn's environment is conducive to life
Saturn is a massive ball made up mostly of nitrogen.
questions.explanation: The correct statement is Option 1: "Satellites like Titan and Enceladus are home to internal oceans and could possibly support life." Both Titan and Enceladus, moons of Saturn, have shown evidence of subsurface oceans beneath their icy exteriors. Enceladus, for example, exhibits geysers that eject water vapor and ice particles, suggesting a liquid water ocean that might harbour conditions favourable to life. Similarly, Titan is believed to have an internal ocean along with a complex organic chemistry, making it a prime candidate for further exploration in the search for extraterrestrial life.

questions.question: Which of the following is a selective credit instrument?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is rationing of credit. This is a selective credit instrument because it is used to channel credit to specific sectors or purposes rather than affecting the entire banking system uniformly. In contrast, instruments like the CRR, bank rate, and variable reserve ratio are general tools that influence overall liquidity and credit availability in the economy.

questions.question: Which country won the inaugural Kho Kho World Cup in both men’s and women’s category?
questions.explanation: India won the inaugural Kho Kho World Cup in both men's and women's categories. The tournament took place from January 13 to 19, 2025, at the Indira Gandhi Indoor Stadium in New Delhi, featuring 23 countries across six continents, with 20 men's and 19 women's teams competing in the fast-paced seven-a-side format.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are CORRECT? I. Change in the magnetic field produces induced current. II. Flow of current in a conductor produces magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The area around a magnet that exerts a force on another magnet or magnetic material is called a magnetic field. A current is induced by a change in the magnetic field, and currents flowing through a conductor produce a magnetic field. Hence, both statements are correct.

Find the interest accrued on a sum of Rs.1,600 in 4 years and 8 months, at the rate of 4.375% simple interest per annum. > 1,600 रुपये की राशि पर 4 वर्ष और 8 महीने में 4.375% प्रति वर्ष साधारण ब्याज की दर से अर्जित ब्याज ज्ञात कीजिए।
Three dices are rolled out and 3 coins are tossed. What is the probability of getting same number on each dice and exactly one head on the coins?
questions.explanation: To calculate this probability, we need to find the probability of getting the same number on each dice and exactly 1 head on the coin, and then multiply these probabilities together. There are 6 possible outcomes where all three dice show the same number {1,1,1}, {2,2,2}, ..., {6,6,6}. Total possible outcomes for three dice = 63 = 216. Probability = 6/216 = 1/36. And, there are 3 possible outcomes with exactly 1 head [HTT, THT, TTH]. Total possible outcomes for three coins = 23 = 8. Probability = 3/8. So, the probability of getting the same number on each dice and exactly 1 head on the coin = 1/36 × 3/8 = 3/288 = 1/96.

questions.question: If the point of intersection of lines 4x - 3y + 9 = 0 and 5x + 2y - 29 = 0 lie on y = Mx + 16, what is the value of M?
questions.explanation: 4x - 3y + 9 = 0 5x + 2y - 29 = 0 On solving these two equations, we get x = 3 and y = 7 Intersection points of above lines are (3, 7). Also, it is given that (3, 7) lies on y = Mx + 16 Put, x = 3 and y = 7 in the given equation, 7 = 3M + 16 3M = -9 M = -3

questions.question: A work can be done by a man, a woman and a child in 12, 8 and 48 days respectively. In how many days will the work be done by 3 men and 1 woman and 6 children?

questions.explanation: 3πr2 Area = 3 × π × 5 × 5 Cost = Area × rate =3 × π × 25× =2775

questions.question: In a 100m race, A beats B by 25 m and B beats C by 40 m. fine the distance by which A beats C. > 100 मीटर की दौड़ में A, B को 25 मीटर से हराता है और B, C को 40 मीटर से हराता है। ज्ञात कीजिए कि A, C को कितनी दूरी से हराता है।

The given table shows the marks obtained by 100 students out of 50 marks in Mathematics and Physics in an examination. If at least 60% marks are required for pursuing higher studies in any subject, what is the mean of no. of students those pursue higher studies in Physics and Mathematics? > दी गई तालिका एक परीक्षा में 100 विद्यार्थियों द्वारा गणित और भौतिकी में 50 में से प्राप्त अंकों को दर्शाती है। यदि किसी भी विषय में उच्च अध्ययन करने के लिए कम से कम 60% अंक आवश्यक हैं, तो भौतिकी और गणित में उच्च अध्ययन करने वाले छात्रों की संख्या का औसत क्या है?
questions.explanation: 60% mark in physics and Mathematics =32+21 Mean = = 26.5 = 27 [Acc.to table] > भौतिकी और गणित में 60% अंक =32+21 माध्य = = 26.5 = 27 [Acc.to table]

questions.question: In an election between two candidates, 40% voters did not cast their votes and 200 votes were declared invalid. The winner got 63% of the valid votes. If he won by 1040 votes, then find the total votes.
questions.explanation: 26% ⟶1040 1 ⟶40 Valid votes = 4000 total given votes =4000+200= 4200 Total votes = ×100 = 7000

questions.question: The ratio of the incomes of A and B is 1:2 and that of their expenditure is 2:3. If 80% of B’s expenditure is equal to the income of A, then what is the ratio of the savings of B to the savings of A?
questions.explanation: Acc. To que = 3y× =x x:y=12:5 Saving = A: B = 2:9 Ans B: A = 9:2

questions.question: What is the single discount equivalent to the successive discounts of 20%, 35%, and 10%?

From a container having pure milk, 30% is replaced by water and the process is repeated twice. At the end of the second operation, the percentage of the water is: > शुद्ध दूध वाले एक बर्तन से 30% पानी की जगह ले ली जाती है और यह प्रक्रिया दो बार दोहराई जाती है। दूसरी प्रक्रिया के अंत में, पानी का प्रतिशत है:

The speed of a train including stoppages is 72 kmph and excluding stoppages, the speed of the train is 80 kmph. For how many minutes does the train stop per hour? > एक रेलगाड़ी की गति ठहरावों सहित 72 किमी प्रति घंटा है तथा ठहरावों को छोड़कर, रेलगाड़ी की गति 80 किमी प्रति घंटा है। रेलगाड़ी प्रति घंटे कितने मिनट रुकती है?
questions.explanation: 80 km in ____ 60 min, 72 km in ____ ×72 = 54 min So 6 minutes train stop per hour > 80 किमी ____ 60 मिनट में, 72 किमी ____ ×72 = 54 मिनट तो प्रति घंटे 6 मिनट ट्रेन रुकती है

questions.question: What will be the amount of debt that is discharged by five equal monthly instalments of ₹3690 each, at the rate of 48% simple interest per annum?
questions.explanation: 48% per annum R= Per month = =4% let instalment =100 100+4 100+8 100+12 100+16 Total = 540⟶ ×540 =19926

questions.question: India will keep purchasing oil from Russia despite U.S. President Donald Trump's threats of penalties, two Indian government sources told Reuters on Saturday, not wishing to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter. On top of a new 25% tariff on India's exports to the U.S., Trump indicated in a Truth Social post last month that India would face additional penalties for purchases of Russian arms and oil. On Friday, Trump told reporters he had heard that India would no longer be buying oil from Russia. But the sources said there would be no immediate changes. "These are long-term oil contracts," one of the sources said. "It is not so simple to just stop buying overnight." Justifying India's oil purchases from Russia, a second source said India's imports of Russian grades had helped avoid a global surge in oil prices, which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector. Unlike Iranian and Venezuelan oil, Russian crude is not subject to direct sanctions, and India is buying it below the current price cap fixed by the European Union, the source said. The New York Times also quoted two unnamed senior Indian officials on Saturday as saying there had been no change in Indian government policy. Indian government authorities did not respond to Reuters' request for official comment on its oil purchasing intentions. However, during a regular press briefing on Friday, foreign ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India has a "steady and time-tested partnership" with Russia. "On our energy sourcing requirements ... we look at what is there available in the markets, what is there on offer, and also what is the prevailing global situation or circumstances," he said. The White House did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Trump, who has made ending Russia's war in Ukraine a priority of his administration since returning to office this year, has expressed growing impatience with Russian President Vladimir Putin in recent weeks. He has threatened 100% tariffs on U.S. imports from countries that buy Russian oil unless Moscow reaches a major peace deal with Ukraine. Russia is the leading supplier to India, the world's third-largest oil importer and consumer, accounting for about 35% of its overall supplies. India imported about 1.75 million barrels per day of Russian oil from January to June this year, up 1% from a year ago, according to data provided to Reuters by sources. But while the Indian government may not be deterred by Trump's threats, sources told Reuters this week that Indian state refiners stopped buying Russian oil after July discounts narrowed to their lowest since 2022 - when sanctions were first imposed on Moscow - due to lower Russian exports and steady demand. Indian Oil Corp, Hindustan Petroleum Corp, Bharat Petroleum Corp and Mangalore Refinery Petrochemical Ltd have not sought Russian crude in the past week or so, four sources told Reuters. Nayara Energy - a refinery majority-owned by Russian entities, including oil major Rosneft, and major buyer of Russian oil - was recently sanctioned by the EU. Nayara's chief executive resigned following the sanctions, and three vessels laden with oil products from Nayara Energy have yet to discharge their cargoes, hindered by the new EU sanctions, Reuters reported last week. Which of the following is the most appropriate title for the passage?
options: Geopolitical Tensions Rise as India Ends Reliance on Russian Oil
Strategic Oil Decisions: India Balances U.S. Sanctions and Russian Partnerships
India Complies with U.S. Demands on Russian Oil Trade
Shifting Trade Routes: India Looks to Alternatives Amid Russian Oil Sanctions
questions.explanation: The Correct option is B because – • The passage clearly shows India is not ending its oil imports from Russia, despite U.S. President Donald Trump's threats of penalties and tariffs. • India is navigating a delicate geopolitical situation — maintaining its long-term oil agreements with Russia while facing diplomatic and economic pressure from the U.S. • The phrase "balances U.S. sanctions and Russian partnerships" captures the core tension and strategic decision-making India is engaged in. • It reflects both the foreign policy dimension (relationship with Russia and the U.S.) and the energy policy aspect (deciding from where to import oil under market and diplomatic pressures). ❌ Why the other options are incorrect: A - Geopolitical Tensions Rise as India Ends Reliance on Russian Oil • This is factually incorrect based on the passage. • India has not ended its reliance on Russian oil — in fact, it's continuing purchases due to long-term contracts and favourable pricing. • The passage explicitly says “there would be no immediate changes.” C - India Complies with U.S. Demands on Russian Oil Trade • Again, this is inaccurate. • India is not complying with U.S. demands. • While there is acknowledgment of U.S. pressure, Indian officials clearly state their independent stance and explain their reasons for continuing oil trade with Russia. D - Shifting Trade Routes: India Looks to Alternatives Amid Russian Oil Sanctions • This suggests that India is actively shifting away from Russian oil, which is only partially true. • Although some state refiners have paused purchases due to pricing, the overall government policy remains unchanged. • Also, Russian oil is not under direct sanctions, which the passage highlights as a justification for continuing imports.

questions.question: India will keep purchasing oil from Russia despite U.S. President Donald Trump's threats of penalties, two Indian government sources told Reuters on Saturday, not wishing to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter. On top of a new 25% tariff on India's exports to the U.S., Trump indicated in a Truth Social post last month that India would face additional penalties for purchases of Russian arms and oil. On Friday, Trump told reporters he had heard that India would no longer be buying oil from Russia. But the sources said there would be no immediate changes. "These are long-term oil contracts," one of the sources said. "It is not so simple to just stop buying overnight." Justifying India's oil purchases from Russia, a second source said India's imports of Russian grades had helped avoid a global surge in oil prices, which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector. Unlike Iranian and Venezuelan oil, Russian crude is not subject to direct sanctions, and India is buying it below the current price cap fixed by the European Union, the source said. The New York Times also quoted two unnamed senior Indian officials on Saturday as saying there had been no change in Indian government policy. Indian government authorities did not respond to Reuters' request for official comment on its oil purchasing intentions. However, during a regular press briefing on Friday, foreign ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India has a "steady and time-tested partnership" with Russia. "On our energy sourcing requirements ... we look at what is there available in the markets, what is there on offer, and also what is the prevailing global situation or circumstances," he said. The White House did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Trump, who has made ending Russia's war in Ukraine a priority of his administration since returning to office this year, has expressed growing impatience with Russian President Vladimir Putin in recent weeks. He has threatened 100% tariffs on U.S. imports from countries that buy Russian oil unless Moscow reaches a major peace deal with Ukraine. Russia is the leading supplier to India, the world's third-largest oil importer and consumer, accounting for about 35% of its overall supplies. India imported about 1.75 million barrels per day of Russian oil from January to June this year, up 1% from a year ago, according to data provided to Reuters by sources. But while the Indian government may not be deterred by Trump's threats, sources told Reuters this week that Indian state refiners stopped buying Russian oil after July discounts narrowed to their lowest since 2022 - when sanctions were first imposed on Moscow - due to lower Russian exports and steady demand. Indian Oil Corp, Hindustan Petroleum Corp, Bharat Petroleum Corp and Mangalore Refinery Petrochemical Ltd have not sought Russian crude in the past week or so, four sources told Reuters. Nayara Energy - a refinery majority-owned by Russian entities, including oil major Rosneft, and major buyer of Russian oil - was recently sanctioned by the EU. Nayara's chief executive resigned following the sanctions, and three vessels laden with oil products from Nayara Energy have yet to discharge their cargoes, hindered by the new EU sanctions, Reuters reported last week. Sentence: "Which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector." What kind of clause is this?
questions.explanation: What is a Clause? - A clause is a group of words that contains a subject and a verb. There are two types of clause - • Independent clause: Makes complete sense on its own. 👉 "India is buying oil." • Dependent clause: Has a subject and verb but cannot stand alone. What is a Relative Clause? A Relative Clause is a type of dependent clause. It begins with a relative pronoun like who, whom, whose, that, which. It describes or gives more information about a noun in the main sentence (just like an adjective does). Back to the Sentence: "Which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector." The clause: "Which have remained subdued..." is giving extra info about oil prices. This makes it a Relative Clause that modifies the noun "oil prices". Let’s visit other options: A. Noun Clause ❌ A noun clause acts as a subject or object (e.g., "What you said was true."). But this clause describes “oil prices,” not acting as subject/object. B. Adverbial Clause ❌ Adverbial clauses explain when, where, why, how about a verb. This clause is not modifying a verb, it’s modifying a noun (oil prices). D. Participial Phrase ❌ That would look like: "Remaining subdued, oil prices..." But here we have a full clause i.e. subject = which, verb = have remained, not just a phrase.

Justifying India's oil purchases from Russia, a second source said India's imports of Russian grades had helped avoid a global surge in oil prices, which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector. Unlike Iranian and Venezuelan oil, Russian crude is not subject to direct sanctions, and India is buying it below the current price cap fixed by the European Union, the source said. The New York Times also quoted two unnamed senior Indian officials on Saturday as saying there had been no change in Indian government policy. Indian government authorities did not respond to Reuters' request for official comment on its oil purchasing intentions. However, during a regular press briefing on Friday, foreign ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India has a "steady and time-tested partnership" with Russia. "On our energy sourcing requirements ... we look at what is there available in the markets, what is there on offer, and also what is the prevailing global situation or circumstances," he said. The White House did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Trump, who has made ending Russia's war in Ukraine a priority of his administration since returning to office this year, has expressed growing impatience with Russian President Vladimir Putin in recent weeks. He has threatened 100% tariffs on U.S. imports from countries that buy Russian oil unless Moscow reaches a major peace deal with Ukraine. Russia is the leading supplier to India, the world's third-largest oil importer and consumer, accounting for about 35% of its overall supplies. India imported about 1.75 million barrels per day of Russian oil from January to June this year, up 1% from a year ago, according to data provided to Reuters by sources. Indian Oil Corp, Hindustan Petroleum Corp, Bharat Petroleum Corp and Mangalore Refinery Petrochemical Ltd have not sought Russian crude in the past week or so, four sources told Reuters. Nayara Energy - a refinery majority-owned by Russian entities, including oil major Rosneft, and major buyer of Russian oil - was recently sanctioned by the EU. Nayara's chief executive resigned following the sanctions, and three vessels laden with oil products from Nayara Energy have yet to discharge their cargoes, hindered by the new EU sanctions, Reuters reported last week. Which of the following statements CANNOT be inferred from the passage?
options: India’s long-term oil contracts with Russia make it difficult to halt purchases immediately.
Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with countries continuing trade with Russia.
All Indian oil refiners have completely stopped importing Russian oil as of July.
Indian officials believe that importing Russian oil has helped stabilize global oil prices.
questions.explanation: The passage says some state refiners have stopped or slowed purchases due to reduced discounts. It mentions: "Indian state refiners stopped buying Russian oil after July discounts narrowed..." • But it does not confirm that ALL refiners have stopped. • Nayara Energy is still mentioned as having shipments impacted by sanctions -suggesting activity is ongoing.

questions.question: India will keep purchasing oil from Russia despite U.S. President Donald Trump's threats of penalties, two Indian government sources told Reuters on Saturday, not wishing to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter. On top of a new 25% tariff on India's exports to the U.S., Trump indicated in a Truth Social post last month that India would face additional penalties for purchases of Russian arms and oil. On Friday, Trump told reporters he had heard that India would no longer be buying oil from Russia. But the sources said there would be no immediate changes. "These are long-term oil contracts," one of the sources said. "It is not so simple to just stop buying overnight." Justifying India's oil purchases from Russia, a second source said India's imports of Russian grades had helped avoid a global surge in oil prices, which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector. Unlike Iranian and Venezuelan oil, Russian crude is not subject to direct sanctions, and India is buying it below the current price cap fixed by the European Union, the source said. The New York Times also quoted two unnamed senior Indian officials on Saturday as saying there had been no change in Indian government policy. Indian government authorities did not respond to Reuters' request for official comment on its oil purchasing intentions. However, during a regular press briefing on Friday, foreign ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India has a "steady and time-tested partnership" with Russia. "On our energy sourcing requirements ... we look at what is there available in the markets, what is there on offer, and also what is the prevailing global situation or circumstances," he said. The White House did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Trump, who has made ending Russia's war in Ukraine a priority of his administration since returning to office this year, has expressed growing impatience with Russian President Vladimir Putin in recent weeks. He has threatened 100% tariffs on U.S. imports from countries that buy Russian oil unless Moscow reaches a major peace deal with Ukraine. Russia is the leading supplier to India, the world's third-largest oil importer and consumer, accounting for about 35% of its overall supplies. India imported about 1.75 million barrels per day of Russian oil from January to June this year, up 1% from a year ago, according to data provided to Reuters by sources. But while the Indian government may not be deterred by Trump's threats, sources told Reuters this week that Indian state refiners stopped buying Russian oil after July discounts narrowed to their lowest since 2022 - when sanctions were first imposed on Moscow - due to lower Russian exports and steady demand. Indian Oil Corp, Hindustan Petroleum Corp, Bharat Petroleum Corp and Mangalore Refinery Petrochemical Ltd have not sought Russian crude in the past week or so, four sources told Reuters. Nayara Energy - a refinery majority-owned by Russian entities, including oil major Rosneft, and major buyer of Russian oil - was recently sanctioned by the EU. Nayara's chief executive resigned following the sanctions, and three vessels laden with oil products from Nayara Energy have yet to discharge their cargoes, hindered by the new EU sanctions, Reuters reported last week. Sentence from the passage: "Trump told reporters he had heard that India would no longer be buying oil from Russia." Which of the following statements is grammatically correct if converted into direct speech?
options: Trump said to reporters, “I hear India will not be buying oil from Russia.”
Trump said to reporters, “I had heard India would no longer be buying oil from Russia.”
Trump said to reporters, “I have heard India will no longer be buying oil from Russia.”
Trump said to reporters, “I heard that India would no longer be buying oil from Russia.”
questions.explanation: In the original sentence, “he had heard” is in past perfect, but in direct speech, past simple (“heard”) is more natural and accepted. D retains correct tense logic, while still maintaining meaning. A and C distort the tense sequence (mixing present and future), and B is unnecessarily formal or awkward in direct speech.

questions.question: India will keep purchasing oil from Russia despite U.S. President Donald Trump's threats of penalties, two Indian government sources told Reuters on Saturday, not wishing to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter. On top of a new 25% tariff on India's exports to the U.S., Trump indicated in a Truth Social post last month that India would face additional penalties for purchases of Russian arms and oil. On Friday, Trump told reporters he had heard that India would no longer be buying oil from Russia. But the sources said there would be no immediate changes. "These are long-term oil contracts," one of the sources said. "It is not so simple to just stop buying overnight." Justifying India's oil purchases from Russia, a second source said India's imports of Russian grades had helped avoid a global surge in oil prices, which have remained subdued despite Western curbs on the Russian oil sector. Unlike Iranian and Venezuelan oil, Russian crude is not subject to direct sanctions, and India is buying it below the current price cap fixed by the European Union, the source said. The New York Times also quoted two unnamed senior Indian officials on Saturday as saying there had been no change in Indian government policy. Indian government authorities did not respond to Reuters' request for official comment on its oil purchasing intentions. However, during a regular press briefing on Friday, foreign ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India has a "steady and time-tested partnership" with Russia. "On our energy sourcing requirements ... we look at what is there available in the markets, what is there on offer, and also what is the prevailing global situation or circumstances," he said. The White House did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Trump, who has made ending Russia's war in Ukraine a priority of his administration since returning to office this year, has expressed growing impatience with Russian President Vladimir Putin in recent weeks. He has threatened 100% tariffs on U.S. imports from countries that buy Russian oil unless Moscow reaches a major peace deal with Ukraine. Russia is the leading supplier to India, the world's third-largest oil importer and consumer, accounting for about 35% of its overall supplies. India imported about 1.75 million barrels per day of Russian oil from January to June this year, up 1% from a year ago, according to data provided to Reuters by sources. But while the Indian government may not be deterred by Trump's threats, sources told Reuters this week that Indian state refiners stopped buying Russian oil after July discounts narrowed to their lowest since 2022 - when sanctions were first imposed on Moscow - due to lower Russian exports and steady demand. Indian Oil Corp, Hindustan Petroleum Corp, Bharat Petroleum Corp and Mangalore Refinery Petrochemical Ltd have not sought Russian crude in the past week or so, four sources told Reuters. Nayara Energy - a refinery majority-owned by Russian entities, including oil major Rosneft, and major buyer of Russian oil - was recently sanctioned by the EU. Nayara's chief executive resigned following the sanctions, and three vessels laden with oil products from Nayara Energy have yet to discharge their cargoes, hindered by the new EU sanctions, Reuters reported last week. Sentence from the passage: "Three vessels laden with oil products from Nayara Energy have yet to discharge their cargoes." Which of the following best explains the role of "laden with oil products from Nayara Energy"?
options: It's an adverbial clause modifying the verb "discharge."
It's a prepositional phrase acting as the main subject.
questions.explanation: A Participial Phrase is a group of words that: • Begins with a participle (present or past) • Functions like an adjective (describes a noun or pronoun) • May include objects or modifiers along with the participle ✅ Structure: [Participle] + [Object/Modifiers] ✅ Function: Describes or gives more info about a noun 🔸 Examples: 1. Present participial phrase - 👉 “Running down the street, the boy tripped and fell.” • “Running down the street” = participial phrase • Describes “the boy” • "Running" = participle • "down the street" = modifier 2. Past participial phrase - 👉 “Shocked by the news, she couldn’t speak.” • “Shocked by the news” = participial phrase • Describes “she” • "Shocked" = participle • "by the news" = modifier Back to our Question - “Laden with oil products from Nayara Energy” is a past participial phrase that describes “vessels.” It acts as an adjective, answering: What kind of vessels? It doesn’t describe the verb “discharge” so not adverbial, nor is it a clause - no subject + verb.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: complete the project Explanation: • The phrase "complete the project" is incorrect in this context because it follows the preposition "of". • According to grammar rules, when a verb follows a preposition, it must be in the -ing [gerund] form. • "Capable of" is a fixed phrase that must be followed by a gerund [verb+ing]. Corrected Sentence: "She is capable of completing the project before the deadline."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Only when __________ failed, the leaders resorted to military intervention.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Persuasions Explanation: The word "persuasions" is the most appropriate choice here. It refers to attempts to convince or influence others, which fits perfectly with the context of the leaders resorting to military intervention after those efforts fail. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Arrests – गिरफ्तारी – The act of detaining someone, typically by law enforcement, due to criminal behaviour. Example: "The police made several arrests during the protest." • Manipulation – नियंत्रण/छल – The action of influencing someone, often in a deceptive or unfair manner. Example: "Manipulation is usually done to control someone's actions for selfish reasons." • Challenges – चुनौतियाँ – Difficulties or obstacles that require effort to overcome. Example: "Overcoming challenges often leads to personal growth." • Persuasions – मनाना/प्रभाव डालना – Efforts to convince or influence someone, typically through reasoning or argument. Example: " His persuasions failed to convince the board, so they had to take a different approach." Final Correct Sentence: "Only when persuasions failed, the leaders resorted to military intervention."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. John didn’t read this book last night, nor Sarah did.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: nor did Sarah Explanation: • The original sentence "John didn’t read this book last night, nor Sarah did" is grammatically incorrect due to wrong word order after "nor." • In formal English, when using "nor" after a negative clause, the correct structure is inverted — that means the auxiliary verb ["did"] comes before the subject ["Sarah"]. Here are the options explained: • [A] and neither Sarah – Incorrect; incomplete structure. It should say "neither did Sarah" to be correct. • [B] and Sarah didn’t too – Incorrect; "didn’t too" is grammatically wrong. It should be "didn’t either," but even then, "nor did Sarah" is better in this formal structure. Final Correct Sentence: "John didn’t read this book last night, nor did Sarah."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: The few knowledge he had was enough to get by.
A few knowledge he had was enough to get by.
The little knowledge he had was enough to get by.
Little knowledge he had was enough to get by.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The little knowledge he had was enough to get by. Explanation: • "The little" is used with uncountable nouns like "knowledge" to refer to a small amount of something, emphasizing that it's the only knowledge he had. • "Little" without an article would sound incomplete in this context since it's missing a determiner. • "A few" and "The few" are used with countable nouns, so they don't work with "knowledge," which is uncountable.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. We didn’t expect much, but the party turned over to be really fun.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Turned out Explanation: The phrasal verb "turned out" is the most appropriate choice here. It means to happen or develop in a particular way, especially one that is unexpected or surprising. In this sentence, it refers to the party ending up being really fun, which fits the intended meaning. Here are the meanings and examples of all the phrasal verbs: • Turned out – घटना या परिणाम होना – To end or result in a particular way. Example: "Despite the clouds, the day turned out sunny." • Turned in – सोने जाना या जमा करना – To go to bed or to submit something. Example: "I turned in my homework on time." • Turned up – पहुंचना या तेज करना – To arrive or to increase volume/level. Example: "He turned up late to the meeting." • Turned over – पलटना या सौंप देना – To flip or hand something over. Example: "She turned over the keys to the new owner." Corrected Sentence: "We didn’t expect much, but the party turned out to be really fun."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Her parents disapproved ____ her decision to quit college.
questions.explanation: Correct answer: Of Explanation: • The verb "disapprove" is always followed by the preposition "of" when expressing disagreement or disapproval about something or someone. • Using "for," "at," or "on" with "disapprove" is grammatically incorrect in this context. Example: "Many people disapprove of smoking in public places." Corrected Sentence: Her parents disapproved of her decision to quit college.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: She did nothing but sang. CONCEPT: When using the structure "do nothing but", the verb that follows must be in its base form [V1], not in the past tense. This is because "but" here functions like "except to", and after such expressions, we use the bare infinitive [base verb without "to"]. Correct sentence: "She did nothing but sing."

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. It encourages awareness and action for protecting the environment worldwide. B. Celebrated every year on June 5, World Environment Day was established by the United Nations in 1972. C. Each year, the day focuses on a different theme related to environmental issues. D. It serves as a global platform for raising environmental concerns and finding solutions.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: BADC Explanation: Let’s understand the logical order: • B introduces the topic – "Celebrated every year on June 5..." – this sets the context. • A explains its purpose – "It encourages awareness and action..." • D adds how it functions – "It serves as a global platform..." • C concludes with a detail – "Each year, the day focuses on a different theme..." Final paragraph: Celebrated every year on June 5, World Environment Day was established by the United Nations in 1972. It encourages awareness and action for protecting the environment worldwide. It serves as a global platform for raising environmental concerns and finding solutions. Each year, the day focuses on a different theme related to environmental issues.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The child laughed at the monkey.
options: The monkey was laughed by the child.
The monkey was being laughed at by the child.
The monkey was laughed at by the child.
The monkey was being laughed by the child.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • Original Sentence [Active Voice]: The child laughed at the monkey. • Subject: The child • Verb: laughed at [a phrasal verb] • Object: the monkey • Passive Voice Rule for phrasal verbs: When converting to passive voice, phrasal verbs like "laugh at" must stay together, and "at" should not be dropped. • Correct Passive Structure: Object + was/were + past participle + preposition + by + subject → The monkey + was laughed at + by the child Final Passive Voice Sentence: The monkey was laughed at by the child.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said to me, “You need not worry.”
options: He said to me that he need not worry.
He told me that I shall need not worry.
He said to me that you need not worry.
He assured me that I need not worry.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence is in direct speech: He said to me, “You need not worry.” • The sentence is meant to offer comfort or reassurance, not just report speech. So, the reporting verb “said to” is changed to “assured” to reflect this tone. • Pronoun change: • “You” becomes “I” [since the speaker is talking to me]. • Modal verb “need not” remains unchanged in indirect speech. Final Indirect Sentence: He assured me that I need not worry.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Barren Let’s see the meaning of all words: Gaeity - Gaiety - उल्लास - A state of joyful celebration or festivity. Example: The gaiety of the festival was infectious. Barren - बंजर - Lacking vegetation or fertility, often used to describe land or soil that is unproductive or unable to support plant life. Example: The barren landscape stretched as far as the eye could see. Assasin - Assassin - हत्यारा - A person who murders someone, especially a prominent or important person, usually for payment or as part of a plot. Example: The assassin was caught and brought to justice. Cemetry - Cemetery - कब्रिस्तान - A place where dead bodies are buried, often with gravestones or other memorials. Example: The old cemetery was said to be haunted.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – I had to jostle my way through the crowded market to find the stall I was looking for.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Shove Let’s see the meaning of all words: Jostle - धक्का-मुक्की करना - To push or shove roughly against someone in a crowd. Example - I had to jostle my way through the crowded market to find the stall I was looking for. Fierce - भयंकर - Showing a strong and powerful quality, often in a way that is frightening or intimidating. Example: The fierce lion roared loudly. Move over - आगे बढ़ना या एक तरफ होना - To change position or make space for someone or something else. Example: Can you move over and let me sit down? Shove - धकेलना - To push someone or something roughly or violently. Example: He shoved me out of the way. Slug - घोंघा या धीमी गति से चलने वाला व्यक्ति - A type of mollusc or a slow-moving person; can also mean to hit someone with a fist. Example: The slug moved slowly across the garden.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Moot
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Incontrovertible Let’s see the meaning of all words: Moot - संदिग्ध या विवादित - Debatable, questionable, or uncertain. Example - Whether the new policy will be effective is a moot point, as we need more data to determine its impact. Debatable - विवादित - Open to discussion or argument; not settled or decided. Example: The topic of climate change is highly debatable, with many experts presenting different viewpoints on its causes and effects, and the debate continues to rage on in the scientific community. Venomous - विषाक्त - Full of malice or spite; poisonous. Example: The venomous snake's bite can be deadly if not treated promptly, and its venom is highly toxic, causing severe pain and swelling in its victims. Woe - दुःख - Great sorrow or distress. Example: The news of the natural disaster brought immense woe to the affected families, who lost their homes and livelihoods, and the community came together to provide support and aid to those in need. Incontrovertible - निर्विवादित - Impossible to dispute or deny; undeniable. Example: The DNA evidence presented in court was incontrovertible, and it clearly linked the defendant to the crime scene, leaving no room for doubt or debate about their guilt.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Shri Ram belonged to an impressive_____________ of great kings.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Pedigree Let’s see the meaning of all words: Sapience - बुद्धिमत्ता - Wisdom or discernment Example: The philosopher's sapience was renowned for its depth and insight, guiding many on the path to enlightenment through their teachings and writings. Pedigree - वंशावली - Lineage or ancestry Example: The royal family's pedigree dates back centuries, with each generation adding to the legacy of their noble lineage through their deeds and accomplishments. Aberrant - असामान्य - Deviating from the norm Example: The aberrant behaviour of the experiment's results required further investigation to understand the anomalies and determine their cause. Benevolent - दयालु - Showing kindness and generosity Example: The benevolent philanthropist donated millions to charity, touching the lives of countless individuals and bringing hope and relief to those in need.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Beside the mark
options: To be in a loss or debt
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Irrelevant Let’s know the meaning of idiom: Beside the mark - लक्ष्य से बाहर - Not hitting the target; figuratively, not relevant or accurate. Example - The comment made by the speaker was beside the mark and did not address the main issue.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Gigantic
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Bulky Let’s see the meaning of all words: Gigantic - विशाल - Extremely large or enormous. Example - The gigantic statue towered over the city, its massive size and intricate details making it a breathtaking sight that drew tourists from all over the world. Flair - विशिष्ट शैली - A natural talent or aptitude for something. Example: She has a flair for painting and creates beautiful artworks. Bulky - भारी और बड़ा - Large and imposing in size; cumbersome. Example: The bulky furniture was difficult to move and required extra effort. Jovial - प्रसन्नचित्त - Good-humoured and cheerful. Example: The jovial host made everyone feel welcome and at ease. Opener - आरंभ करने वाला - Something that opens or begins something else. Example: The opener for the bottle was stuck and wouldn't work properly.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Suresh made a bequest of his paintings to the museum.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Acquisition Let’s see the meaning of all words: Bequest - विरासत - A gift or legacy left to someone in a will. Example - The philanthropist's bequest to the university funded a scholarship program for underprivileged students, providing them with opportunities for higher education and a brighter future. Destitute - दरिद्र - Extremely poor or lacking resources. Example: The family was left destitute after losing their home in the natural disaster. Patrimony - पितृक संपत्ति - Property or assets inherited from one's father or ancestors. Example: The ancient castle was part of the family's patrimony for generations. Acquisition - अधिग्रहण - The act of getting or obtaining something. Example: The museum's new acquisition included a rare and valuable painting. Constrict - सिकोड़ना - To tighten or narrow something. Example: The tight bandage started to constrict blood flow to the injured limb

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A book where name and addresses of people living in area is kept
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Directory Let’s see the meaning of all words: Bibliophile - पुस्तक प्रेमी - A person who loves or collects books. Example: The bibliophile spent years accumulating a vast collection of rare and classic literature. Conformist - अनुरूपवादी - A person who conforms to established standards or norms. Example: The conformist followed the rules and regulations without questioning their validity. Cacographer - अशुद्ध लेखक - A person who writes or spells badly. Example: The cacographer's work was riddled with errors and required extensive editing. Directory - निर्देशिका - A book or list containing names, addresses, and contact information of people or organizations. Example: The phone directory listed all the residents and businesses in the area.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A leopard can’t change his spots
options: A person’s basic nature or habits are hard to change
To return to the starting point
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - A person’s basic nature or habits are hard to change Let’s see the meaning of idiom: A leopard can’t change his spots - तेंदुआ अपने धब्बे नहीं बदल सकता - You can't change who you are or your bad habits. Example - He's been dishonest for years, so it's unlikely he'll become trustworthy now. A leopard can't change his spots.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Someone having a many skills
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Versatile Let’s see the meaning of all words: Curator - क्यूरेटर - A person who manages or oversees something, especially a museum or art collection. Example: The curator of the art museum carefully selected pieces for the new exhibit. Utility - उपयोगिता - The state of being useful or practical. Example: The utility of the new software was evident in its ability to streamline workflow. Versatile - बहुमुखी - Having many skills or talents; adaptable. Example: The versatile actor excelled in both comedy and drama roles. Chastise - फटकारना - To criticize or punish someone severely. Example: The teacher chastised the student for not completing their homework.
